accomplishments: Create a button that responds when you click it
Write your first line of JavaScript code

do.title: Make a Button Say Hello
steps: Open VS Code (or go to CodePen.io in your browser)
Create a new file and save it as talking-button.html
Copy and paste the code below
Double-click the file to open it in your browser
Click the button — you'll see a popup appear!
do.code: My First JavaScript
do.result: You just created an interactive button that responds to clicks using JavaScript!
do.proofRequired: Screenshot showing the popup message "Hello! You clicked me!" after clicking your button.

concepts.name: The <button> tag + onclick
concepts.definition: The button creates a clickable element. The onclick attribute tells it what JavaScript to run when clicked.
concepts.analogy: A doorbell button on a house. onclick is the wiring that connects it to the chime.

concepts.name: alert() function
concepts.definition: JavaScript code that shows a popup message. Every browser understands alert(). Great for testing, not great for real websites (popups are annoying).
concepts.analogy: A sticky note that pops up in front of everything until you press OK.

concepts.name: HTML creates elements, JavaScript makes them DO things
concepts.definition: HTML builds the structure (the button). JavaScript adds behavior (what happens when you click). They work together but are different languages.
concepts.analogy: HTML is building a car. JavaScript is the engine that makes it drive.

understand.keyTakeaway: HTML creates elements. JavaScript makes them DO things. onclick + alert() is the simplest way to start.

build.goal: Make the button show a personalized greeting with YOUR name

codeComparison.new:
<button onclick="alert('Hello, Sarah! Welcome to JavaScript!')">Click Me!</button>

<!-- Replace "Sarah" with YOUR actual name! -->

build.hint: Make sure to keep the quotes around your text: alert('like this'), not alert(like this). The quotes tell JavaScript this is text to display.

challenge.title: Create a Compliment Button
challenge.mission: Create a button that gives you a different compliment each time you click it, picked randomly from 3 options.
requirements: Button text says "Give Me a Compliment"
When clicked, shows one of 3 different compliments
Each compliment is encouraging and positive
challenge.starterCode: Daily Compliment Generator
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add 5 compliments instead of 3! Change the random number generator to pick from 1-5.

capabilities: Use onclick to make elements respond to clicks
Use if/else statements to make decisions

bridge.preview: In the next lesson, you'll learn how to change what's on the page WITHOUT annoying popups. You'll make text appear, disappear, and change — all with JavaScript.
